Hello friends, 
 We just added two new ways to keep Whisper Memos organized and connected: Labels and Webhooks. 
 Labels help you group related memos inside the app. Webhooks let you send memos to your own tools, scripts, and internal workflows. 
 Open Whisper Memos 
 Labels 
 You can now create labels like Journal, Ideas, Meetings, Clients, or Workouts, and use them to keep related recordings together. 
 Create them in Settings → Labels . Each label can have its own name, color, and icon, so it is easy to spot in the memo list. 
 Assign labels manually 
 Sometimes you record first and decide where the memo belongs later. For that, long-press any finished memo and choose Assign to Label . 
 Each memo can have one label. You can also clear the label later from the same menu. 
 Labels with agents 
 Labels also work with agents. Add an Assign label step to an agent, and Whisper Memos will apply that label whenever the agent runs. 
 For example, a meeting agent can summarize the memo, send it to email, and label it as Meetings in one workflow. 
 Webhooks 
 Webhooks are for custom workflows. Open Settings → Integrations → Webhooks , add a URL, and Whisper Memos can send new memo data to your endpoint as an HTTP POST request. 
 You can send JSON or plain text. For JSON, you can choose which fields to include and rename the keys, so it can fit the tool on the other side. 
 Getting started 
 Update Whisper Memos and open Settings → Labels or Settings → Integrations → Webhooks . 
 Open Whisper Memos 
 As always, thank you for using the app. If you have a moment, please leave a rating on the App Store . It really helps other people find Whisper Memos. 
 —Vojtech

Hey there,

We shipped three updates this week that give your AI agent better knowledge of your data, a way to keep that knowledge current, and the data to know whether it's working.

Context for connections
-----------------------

You can already give the agent context at workspace, catalog and project level. Now you can add it to individual data connections too, whether that's a database or an MCP ( https://learn.count.co/apis-and-integrations/mcp-connections ) connection. The grain of a table, naming conventions, known quality issues, what a connection should or shouldn't be used for. Anything you'd tell a new analyst before letting them near a source.

Go to any connection, click Manage Connection, and add context there.

Context via the public API
--------------------------

Context used to mean updating things by hand in the Count UI. If your team keeps definitions in dbt YAML, a wiki, a git repo, or Notion, that's unnecessary friction and things drift.

You can now read and write context at any level through the public API. Put it on a schedule, wire it into a CI/CD pipeline, or trigger an update when your data model changes.

More info: API docs ( https://learn.count.co/apis-and-integrations/public-api )

Agent telemetry
---------------

Count telemetry has always covered queries, users, and canvas activity. We've now added agent data. The new agent_history table logs every conversation your agent has, including messages, timestamps, and errors.

Jason and I ran a webinar ( https://count.co/webinar/self-serve-agent-series-part-2-maintain ) on this recently. Here are a few ways we used this telemetry data:

* 📈 To create simple metrics tracking who the AI power users are and whether usage is growing or dropping off week on week.
* 💬 To identify which tables the agent is actually querying. We may spot it hitting staging tables it shouldn't have been, which points to a context gap.
* 🚨 We asked the agent to find conversations where

EMAIL NOTICE If you purchased five or more new physical goods from third-party sellers on Amazon’s marketplace on or after May 26, 2017, you may be a class member in an ongoing class action lawsuit. 
For more information about the lawsuit, read below or visit www.AmazonAntitrustLitigation.com . 
Para una notification en Espanol, visitor www.AmazonAntitrustLitigation.com . A federal court authorized this Notice. This is not a solicitation from a lawyer. 
 Who’s included? You could be affected by a lawsuit against Amazon.com, Inc. The Court ordered this notice and decided that the lawsuit should proceed on behalf of a group of people that could include you (the “Class”). The Court has decided that, unless you request to opt out (exclude yourself), you are a member of the Class defined as: all persons in the United States who on or after May 26, 2017, purchased five or more new, physical goods from third-party sellers on Amazon’s marketplace.
   What is the lawsuit about? The name of the lawsuit is De Coster et al. v. Amazon.com, Inc. , and it is pending in the United States District Court for the Western District of Washington (Case No. 2:21-cv-00693-JHC). 
A group of consumers (“Plaintiffs”) have filed a class action lawsuit against Amazon. The lawsuit alleges that Amazon violated federal antitrust laws by using its market power to impose and enforce an anti-discounting policy on its third-party sellers that allowed Amazon to charge higher commission fees and caused higher prices for goods sold on the Amazon marketplace by third-party sellers. Specifically, Plaintiffs claim that Amazon violated the Sherman Act, 15 U.S.C. § 1 et seq. Plaintiffs are seeking money damages for purchasers and changes to Amazon’s policies and practices. Amazon denies any wrongdoing, and the Court has not yet decided whether Amazon did anything wrong. 
 Who represents the Class? The Court has appointed the law firms of Hagens Berman Sobol Shapiro LLP, Keller Postman LLC, and Quinn Eman

We will publish additional blogs as greater clarity is provided.
First up, the Fed. About a week ago—though it seems a lot longer—the Fed began a new era under the leadership of Kevin. In our opinion, he followed a predictable script. It didn’t surprise us that Warsh—appointed by Trump, who has pushed hard for lower interest rates and a lower dollar—nonetheless took a seemingly hawkish stance. Warsh could not simply have jumped in and immediately lowered interest rates with no rationale. He would have looked like a puppet and lost all credibility. He had to set the stage.
And it seems that Warsh succeeded in coming across as hawkish, as evidenced by the shift in interest rate futures. Expectations now project one or two Fed rate hikes by yearend. Prior to the Fed meeting, the expectations were for no hikes. The seemingly hawkish turn is at least partially responsible for the recent weakness in precious metals and strength in the dollar. But we think that ultimately the opposite will occur. We expect the next interest rate move will be down, not up, despite what Warsh said.
Driving the about-face will be a combination of three factors.
First will be unexpected economic weakness stemming from a near-record-low savings rate of 3%. Behind this low savings rate is consumer spending that has significantly exceeded consumer income. When we chose to name this publication Turbulent Times Investor, we knew the name would be apt. But lately it has been proving even more apt than we could have imagined, as the world undergoes rapid changes that threaten to upend the existing financial order. In the very recent period—with the war in Iran adding fuel to the fire—it feels as if the pace of change has been accelerating, and in just the past few weeks there have been developments with big implications for the U.S. economy, the country’s place in the world, and, not so incidentally, for investors.
The accompanying article (originally a TTI update) focuses on one of these events: the bond auction last month in which the U.S. was forced to offer interest topping 5% on 30-year bonds, a nearly unheard-of level. It was a clanging alarm bell signifying the pressures mounting on the U.S. and on the dollar as our enormous debt continues to climb. But it has been followed in rapid succession by other notable developments.
For investors, it adds up to a compelling message: Your paramount concern has to be safety. This isn’t the time for heedless greed and chasing after the biggest short-term gains, a pursuit we think will boomerang. Rather, it’s essential that you position yourself to withstand the toughest economic environment—whether that ends up being inflation, deflation, or stagflation, any one of which could emerge—and a sharp market setback. We can’t give a precise timeline, and we can’t say just how severe the market downturn will get. But we expect that a lot of people will experience a lot of pain, and we want to make sure you’re not among them.

SpaceX is going public tomorrow in the biggest initial public offering (IPO) in history. It will be followed shortly by the IPOs of Anthropic and OpenAI. While you’re watching the NBA finals and the World Cup, these companies are rewriting the rules of the stock market game for everyone.
NASDAQ, FTSE Russell, and MSCI all changed their requirements so as to fast-track these forthcoming IPOs. The S&P 500, to its credit, did not. It continues to mandate a one-year “seasoning” period before a company can be considered for inclusion.
But if you’re invested in any of the other indexes, for example, if you have a 401(k) that invests in technology-based index funds, within a mere few weeks you’ll have a stake in SpaceX. NASDAQ has shortened its seasoning period to only 15 days, while FTSE Russell introduced a new fast-entry rule that slashed its waiting period to five trading days. MSCI waived its standard waiting period and float requirements to fast-track large companies in as little as 10 trading days.
Why should this concern you? Because history shows us that large IPOs can experience a fast fall. After racing out of the gate thanks to hype and aggressive initial valuations, they often decline sharply within the following year. Notably, the three AI companies currently in the pipeline already have sold significant shares through private funding rounds. Historically, companies went public early to allow the public to capture the gains of their growth phase. But a good look at the books of these three AI companies reveals stretched valuations that leave less meat on the bone for the public. In other words, much of the money to be made in SpaceX will be claimed not by the people who buy in on Friday, but by the investors and insiders that already own shares.

Hey there,

MCPs as a data source

You can now connect any MCP server to Count as a data source, the same way you'd connect a database.

Slack, HubSpot, Stripe, Linear, Notion, Intercom, Fireflies, Mixpanel: we've built 24 pre-configured integrations, plus a generic option for anything else. Add a connection, assign it to a project, and the agent can pull from it directly in your canvas.

The interesting part: this isn't just more tables. MCP connections can return anything. Support transcripts, company documents, strategy notes, the context scattered across your stack, sitting right next to your numbers and ready to analyse together.

Here are a few use cases to get you started:

* Build a customer 360 that combines usage data with themes pulled from support calls.
* Analyse data in the context of your latest product releases or strategic priorities.
* Connect a third-party semantic layer and blend it with data already in Count.

A few weeks ago we shipped our MCP client, so you could take Count's analysis out into other tools. Now it works both ways.

-> Read the full blog post ( https://count.co/blog/mcps-as-data )

-> Read the docs ( https://learn.count.co/apis-and-integrations/mcp-connections )

Pain and Suffering? The Economic Threat from AI
July 1, 2026 | By Stephen Leeb and Donna Leeb
Among other things, the risk of a market crash
Last month, on June 24 to be precise, two companies released statements that were notable in themselves and even more striking for coming on the same day.
One was the release by memory-chip leader Micron of astoundingly blockbuster fiscal third-quarter earnings. The company reported that revenues had quadrupled year-over-year to $41.46 billion, while earnings soared 1,215% to $28.24 billion. It’s impossible to overstate just how extraordinary these figures are.
Memory chips are essential to virtually all modern technology, used in smartphones, laptops, automobiles, gaming consoles, and much more, but the bulk of demand is coming from the ongoing explosion in the numbers and size of AI data centers. The escalating demand for the chips has led to severe supply shortages and rising prices, fueling Micron’s astounding growth.
The other announcement came from Apple. The company said that the rising costs of components, including memory chips, is forcing it to sharply raise prices on many of its products including laptops and iPads.
Coming on the same day, the two events constitute a kind of microcosm of some of the pressures on the U.S. economy that are accelerating as we move beyond the country’s 250th anniversary. They are part and parcel of developments that suggest that this first year of the next semi-quincentennial could see major cracks in the economy and a severe market downturn. If this assessment sounds bleak, we apologize for perhaps dampening any July 4th celebrations you may be planning, but our goal is to be realistic—and to ensure that you are prepared. Below is a snapshot overview of some of the things that are keeping us up at night.

“Never say never.” That’s a phrase that, despite being self-contradictory in nature, still makes a clear point– at least to those who watched the recent victory of the New York Knicks in the NBA championship finals. It was a triumph for the ages. The team, which had not ascended to the world championship for more than half a century, was captained by a stocky, small guard named Jalen Brunson. Brunson joined the knicks in 2022 and became the team’s captain two years later in 2024. In the two years since Brunson assumed the captaincy, the Knicks have reached the Eastern Conference Finals and captured the NBA title. But the true magic lies in how they did it - an exemplar not just for sports, but for any cohesive unit—from America’s storied corporations to elite military platoons.
They shattered multiple NBA playoff records. They finished with a historic 9-0 record on the road - never dropping a single away game - to set the NBA record for the longest road winning streak in a single postseason. They outscored their playoff opponents by an average of 14.9 points per game, marking the best point differential in NBA playoff history. They also went 6-2 in games where they trailed by double digits, the best such playoff record in the 30 years of recorded play-by-play data. In game 4 of the Finals, they broke the record for largest comeback in NBA Finals history, rallying from a 29-point deficit (with just over 9 minutes left in the 4th quarter, they were still down by 20 points).
Mike Brown, the head coach of the New York Knicks, deserves a lot of credit to be sure. This was his first year at the helm, and he obviously did a tremendous job. But as author Sam Walker points out in his best-selling book, The Captain Class, it is the captain of the team who seems to have the most influence on their success. Despite massive evidence to the contrary, investors seem more willing than ever to bet that AI in the U.S. has limitless potential. A slew of incoming Initial Public Offerings (IPOs) involving AI are about to land, the most fantastic of which is SpaceX, a company completely controlled and captained by Elon Musk. The IPO for SpaceX is set to hit on June 12, and the market is expected to value the company at the stunning sum of around $1.75 trillion.
When you think of SpaceX, you are likely to imagine rocket ships designed for interplanetary travel or spacecraft designed to carry us to the moon and beyond. The more sensible and level-headed among us is likely to think primarily of Starlink, their constellation of satellites that provide internet across the globe.
But shockingly enough, if you read the prospectus that SpaceX filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), you find that SpaceX is marketing itself as an AI company. A prospectus is a formal, usually very boring, document that companies are legally required to present to provide details on their investment offerings to the public.
In looking over the SpaceX prospectus, it is not hard to understand why Starlink is the only subsidiary making money. The other subsidiaries—AI and the space businesses—have combined losses to date totaling $37 billion. But even Starlink shows per-user revenues declining, so it does not take a financial genius to assume that Starlink alone would have a tough, if not impossible, time making up for that $37 billion loss over the foreseeable future. (Patrick Boyle broke the situation down hilariously in his video “Nice Try Though.” We especially enjoyed the comment about the missing money on the accounting sheets falling off the back of a Cybertruck.)
How, then, can SpaceX possibly be poised to garner the highest-ever valuation for an IPO?

With our Urban Month Favorites!


** Glass Highlights
March Edition
------------------------------------------------------------
Recognizing a creative season as it's happening is a special gift. This weekend will mark my first anniversary shooting digital medium format. It completely shifted the type of work I was making and how I was seeing when I went out to photograph. It's wild to think it's only been a year, in the same way that it's wild to think that the third anniversary of Glass is coming up this summer.

What did I do before Glass? Who can say.
https://glass.photo/highlights/mountain-favorites

U (https://glass.photo/highlights/urban-favorites) rban (https://glass.photo/highlights/urban-favorites) was such special Category for me — rare that the Monthly Category is the exact type of photography I shoot, you know? Enjoy your stroll through city living or let me walk with you on the Glass YouTube (https://youtu.be/24WnWgOw1vU) .


**  Highlights
------------------------------------------------------------
* Spotlights with Layla (https://glass.photo/highlights/layla-perchal-neal) , Canyon (https://glass.photo/highlights/canyon-mccarty) , and Jen (https://glass.photo/highlights/spring-details-with-jen) !
* We released our new website (https://glass.photo/highlights/reintroducing-glass) ! It's great!
* We had a great meetup in ATX (https://glass.photo/highlights/austin-glass-meetup) ! More coming soon.
* Spring (https://glass.photo/highlights/spring-category) is April's Monthly Category!


**   Product Announcements
------------------------------------------------------------

We released a new Category picker! Live on Web right now and coming to iOS soon, the Category picker now surfaces your most used Categories so you don't have to scroll through the full list.

In Gym Masses: We begin our Advent season 2023: The People Who Walk In Darkness Will See a Great Light. We will hear stories and songs of light in the darkness. We will breathe into the light of God within us, and we will kindle this light in our weary world. Today we give thanks for Mariella Elm, who will share her story with us!

In Family Mass: Our Advent theme this year is The Darkness Above Us Is Filled With Light. On this First Sunday of Advent, we’re looking for hope in darkness with a story The Christmas Miracle of Jonathan Toomey, read by Tinia Moulder, and joined by our Children’s Choir, which begins its season with “Be a Candle of Hope.” Come each week as we light the Advent Wreath, build the Manger, and watch the birds fill our Advent trees. Be part of the Star Light Tree and take our Jesse Tree adventure.

Full details on our ministries in the bulletin (click above) or on our website (click below).

Mon-Thur, 9am-8pm

Fri, 9am - 3pm

Sat, 9am-Noon

Sun, 8am - 12:30pm

With COVID, and other respiratory ills, we support wearing masks in large indoor gatherings.

** Glass Highlights
November Edition
------------------------------------------------------------
Welcome to the first Weekend Edition™ of the Glass Monthly! (I forgot yesterday.) Will keep this short since no one likes reading (or writing) emails on the weekend.

So many great photographs waiting for you, though.
https://glass.photo/highlights/framed-favorites

The Framed™ Favorites (https://glass.photo/highlights/framed-favorites) were so good this month that I hopped on the Glass Youtube Channel (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bSLuvWxBfAw) to talk about them (plz like and subscribe, thx).


**  Highlights
------------------------------------------------------------
* Spotlights with Lynn (https://glass.photo/highlights/lynn-mctaggart) , Ute (https://glass.photo/highlights/ute-reckhorn) , and Priit (https://glass.photo/highlights/colors-and-composition) !
* Updated Pricing (https://glass.photo/highlights/2024-pricing) for 2024.
* December is for Everyday. (https://glass.photo/highlights/everyday-category)


**   Product Announcements
------------------------------------------------------------

We fixed the Share Sheet on iOS and iPad! When exporting your image from your editor of choice, you can tap Glass and it'll work! Reliably!

This bug has been the bane of my existence since we launched, and getting this fix out feels so good.


**   Did you know?
------------------------------------------------------------

Sharing specific searches from our new Explore is easy! For example, Paris at Night (https://glass.photo/explore/photos/Paris-at-night) . Ugh, now I want to go to Paris.
Paris Glass Meetup 2024?

Daniel (https://glass.photo/daniel) and the Glass team

All the As to your Q's.


** Glass Weekly
024
------------------------------------------------------------

It came up during the Q&A we did this week and so it's been on my brain — this weekend will mark a year since Glass left Twitter. (https://glass.photo/highlights/glass-is-leaving-twitter) There was a ~5% dip in our traffic for the first month or two, and then we normalized. What a difference a year makes, huh?

The longer we work on Glass, the greater I appreciate the part that time plays in building something. Which is an important lesson for me to remember to apply to my photography too.


**   (https://glass.photo/highlights/instant-calm) Spotlight — Heather Polley
------------------------------------------------------------
https://glass.photo/highlights/instant-calm

The only thing that broke during our last move was my Instax camera. Anytime Heather posts a little bit of magic, I remind myself I gotta get that fixed. Because just look at these.

Enjoy her Spotlight (https://glass.photo/highlights/instant-calm) and follow Heather on Glass (https://glass.photo/hpolleyphotography) .


** 📺 YouTube — End-of-Year Q&A
------------------------------------------------------------
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PANqZlA7nVg

You asked thoughtful questions, I tried to provide thoughtful answers (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PANqZlA7nVg) . Sorry about the camera battery dying, I'll spill more secrets next time.
Be kind to yourself moving into the last few weeks of the year. January 1st is an arbitrary and made up deadline and we're all just trying our best.

**   (https://glass.photo/highlights/summer-murdock) Spotlight — Summer Murdock
------------------------------------------------------------
https://glass.photo/highlights/summer-murdock

My first thought any time Summer uploads a photo is "I gotta buy an underwater housing." My second thought, without fail, is "Her photography has so much more going on than just having an underwater housing." It's truly special work — recommend enjoying a scroll through her entire profile.

F (https://glass.photo/highlights/african-portraiture) loat through her Spotlight (https://glass.photo/highlights/summer-murdock) and follow Summer on Glass (https://glass.photo/summer_murdock) .

Introducing the Glass Weekly!


** Glass Highlights
March Edition
------------------------------------------------------------

Introducing the Glass Weekly! Here's the pitch. Every Thursday, we'll send an email with a quick note saying hello, feature a photographer whose work we adore, and a link to that week's episode of Endless Appreciation. That's it. That's Glass Weekly.

Here, we'll show you. This has been the quick note hello. Next up is...


** https://glass.photo/highlights/minimalist-birds-with-jennifer Spotlight — Jennifer Smutek
------------------------------------------------------------
https://glass.photo/highlights/minimalist-birds-with-jennifer

Jennifer is always looking up. Her collection of minimalist birds in cityscapes has been wonderful to see come to life on Glass. See more of her work in our Spotlight (https://glass.photo/highlights/minimalist-birds-with-jennifer) and follow her on Glass (https://glass.photo/j_smutek) .


** Endless Appreciation
------------------------------------------------------------
This week, Sam and I were joined by Tom (https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/endless-appreciation/id1672664101?i=1000608398795) to discuss designing Glass! Ever wondered about the nitty gritty details that go into our decision making and how an indie product gets made?

You don't want to miss this discussion. (https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/endless-appreciation/id1672664101?i=1000608398795)

That's it! That's the Glass Weekly. Our first issue goes out next week. Only want to hear from us once a month? We get it. Click this link to just get the Monthly Highlights email. (https://glass.photo/monthly-newsletter)

Speaking of, let's get back to it.

------------------------------------------------------------

** Design a Birdhouse
------------------------------------------------------------

To celebrate Earth Day, our April drawing challenge is to design a birdhouse. Create a safe and snug house for birds to roost and nest. Birdhouses offer an excellent opportunity for gardeners and nature enthusiasts to make a difference in their local environment. From forests to parks, providing a home for birds ensures populations stay healthy and helps repopulate species that need a hand.

Discover details about the challenge here. (https://concepts.app/en/contest/design-a-birdhouse-challenge/?utm_source=email&utm_medium=newsletter)


** Interview with Epifanio Pereira
------------------------------------------------------------

Epifanio Pereira (http://concepts.app/creators/epifanio-pereira) is a visualisation-focused designer who looks to both sketching and technology when it comes to his creative process. His curiosity lies in exploring the possibilities of innovating User Experience, Product Design and Applied AI.

Read our interview (https://concepts.app/en/stories/visualisation-focused-design) with Epifanio to learn more about his creative process.

Concepts 6 for iOS is coming soon to your iPad & iPhone.

Infinite, flexible sketching.


** Concepts 6 is coming soon.
------------------------------------------------------------

We're looking forward to sharing a new iteration of Concepts with you, coming shortly. Concepts 6 for iOS will have an updated canvas look and feel, be simpler to navigate while retaining its deep flexibility, and offer more organization so you can create more in less time. We hope you'll dive right into your creative flow and mingle with your ideas more naturally.

A few things you can look forward to:
* a new logo to go with the new app feel
* a modernized status bar that places your key interactions at the front of your workflow
* a restructured Gallery integrated with the Files app for better project management
* many improvements to update the speed and flow of your creative experience
* and to celebrate 10 years since we launched in the App Store, we have a surprise new feature coming

We're pleased to share that the updates to the Gallery are another major step towards delivering iCloud sync. We're not quite there yet but with the new gallery, integration with Files, and a lot of work under the hood, we are a giant leap closer to delivering on our promise of full iCloud integration.

Be on the lookout for our Concepts 6 update coming in the next few weeks! Thank you for your excellent insights and feedback that have helped us to come so far. Your support has helped us every day to continue creating.

Cheerfully,

The Concepts Team

Hello everyone!  I'm just thinking about all of you today and encouraging
you to stay with St. Joans this weekend.  *Advent Evensong *is now
available and if you have a half hour to give yourself to the preparning of
your heart for Christmas...this is a beautiful meditation!  The schola
leads the singing and Carin and Tommy do an absolutely stunning version of
"Breath of Heaven."  Our own Karin Grosscup takes us through some quieting
and meaningful meditations while Joan Riebel shares the story of her
waiting during her son, Jack's battle with cancer.  Then...our *Advent
Sundays *are really beautiful.  The schola sings "Now the Day is Over" at
offertory this Sunday.  AND...best of all, tune into the Kevin Kling *"Tales
>From the Charred Underbelly of the Yule **"**Log" *this Sunday evening at
7:00.  Dress up, have a nice beverage and "go to a concert!!"  I love you
all and encourage you to sign up on this page again and share some of your
life with us!
Peace and virtual hugs,
Anna

What if you were to take a vacation to a warm weather destination to feel that sunshine once again?

From: Fr.Jim DeBruycker                                                 September 11, 2020

If you are reading this on Saturday, September 12, here’s a reminder that tomorrow – Sunday, September 13 - is Drive Thru Sunday in the SJA parking lot from 9 to Noon. It’s this year’s “virus motivated” Welcome Back Sunday. Steve Friedrichs and Rose Aspholm, with the help of a number of other staff members and volunteers, have organized the parking lot so you can pick up info on the virtual plans for a lot of our ministries and programs. You will also be able to buy Cabaret raffle tickets and win the use of condo in Maui plus $750 in spending money or you can win one of the two cash prizes - $500 and $250. Once you’ve gone through the parking lot you’ll exit on Third Avenue.

There are two very important ground rules for the drive through. Please wear a mask at all times and please stay in your car at all times. These are both very important for the safety of all of us. We ask you to stay with the marked route which means you’ll come off 46th Street on Clinton, down to 45th where you can enter and then follow the directions around the lot to the exit on Third Avenue. Sorry but we’re not serving coffee and cookies.

The Drive Thru is the way to get information to you – and, quite honestly, to see you, albeit just your eyes above the mask and then we’ll have to guess identities. I’ve really been challenged when I’m walking around the lake or in Target and someone greets by name with a mask covering two-thirds of their face. I try to guess who they are by looking at their eyes. Thanks to those of you who have adopted the very helpful practice of greeting and then saying “It’s ------“.

It’s not breaking news but this weekend marks six months since we’ve had liturgies at SJA. There are few of us of a certain age that could never imagine no Mass for 26 Sundays. So strange! At the sam

From:  Fr. Jim DeBruycker                                           June 5, 2020

For those of you who read the Pastor’s 2 Cents in the online bulletin some of this is going to sound familiar but I do want to reach out to as many parishioners as I can this week.

It’s hard to know where to begin, so much has happened. Of course the murder of George Floyd and the subsequent protests are foremost on our minds. It is almost to the point where you don’t know what to say because it has all been said before: “Black Lives Matter” “We are all one.” So, now it’s time to do something about it. But what?

For openers I’d suggest a good start would be to read Fr. Bryan N. Massingale’s commentary in the National Catholic Reporter, The Assumptions of White Privilege and What we can do about it. If I were your professor I would make it mandatory reading.  You can read it at the National Catholic Reporter website HERE [http://www.ncronline.org/news/opinion/assumptions-white-privilege-and-what-we-can-do-about-it].

It is rare that a video so affects us, but the murder of George Floyd asks an ‘ontological’ why? Why destroy this man for no apparent reason? It is so powerful a question it has moved people of the U.S. and other nations to erupt in protest.  Our president in his own inimitable need to project his own weaknesses unto others answered the question –DOMINATE!

The fear that we might lose our power, our privileges, our control, our status. “You have to dominate; if you don’t dominate you are wasting your time. They are going to run over you. You are going to look like jerks. You have to dominate.” And the Lord said, “Dominate one another, as I have dominated you. Otherwise you look like jerks.”  The very sad truth is the president knows what he is saying and knows who wants to hear it.

Monthly Digest: February 2019

Filled with the latest Intuiface news, tips and information

 

Digital Signage Expo + Interactive 2019 - Vegas Edition

**Visit the Intuiface booth, sample the Intuiface User Conference, or do
both!**

It's a Vegas double-header. From March 27 to 28, Intuiface will
highlight everything interactive in Booth 3240 at the Digital Signage
Expo (DSE) in Las Vegas. And on the morning of March 28, just a short
walk from the DSE Exhibitor Hall, Intuiface will host the Vegas Edition
of their first annual Intuiface User Conference, completed back on
February 6 in Amsterdam. It's a chance for those who missed the big show
to hear the exclusive content that won't go public. 

See all events→  

 

The Intuiface Cloud: What You Need To Know

An inside look at the world you don't see.

Intuiface is not just a pretty face, it's also a world of infrastructure
that is so efficient you probably don't even know it's there. In this
article, we lift the curtain and show you what's going on behind the
scenes. You'll learn not just what cloud-hosted services are making your
life easier, but also how we monitor ourselves to ensure problems are
identified, diagnosed, and fixed as rapidly as possible.

****

Read on→

 

Interactive 2019 and ISE 2019 Recap

**We're exhausted.......**Wow! What a show! A big thank you to all who
attended Interactive 2019 and/or who visited us at ISE on our booth.
Needless to say, the week far exceeded our expectations. Depending on
whether you attended or couldn't make it, this recap will be a fond
reminder of the good times or a tidy review of what we covered. We have
lots of videos to share!

Happy New Year  Women of SJA,

We are preparing for our workshop Self-Care in Troubling Times II on February 3, at 9:00 am.  Our speaker/facilitator Rhonda Battisto will focus on the physical and emotional aspects of stress.  Wear comfortable clothes as movement might be part of the session.  Any movement will not require that you leave your seat if you cannot do so.

Rhonda Battisto is a Spring Forest Qigong (SFQ) Master Healer and Certified Instructor who provides healing support services, workshops, presentations and empowering retreats in the USA and Canada.  With a strong, lifelong interest in natural holistic wellness, Rhonda has studied homeopathy, Craino-sacral Bodywork, Inner Peace, Light Therapy, Reflexology, Guided Imagery, the Wheel of Law, Storytelling, Leadership, Forgiveness, Personal Empowerment, Peace Building, and Authentic Intimacy.  Rhonda has worked closely with International Qigong Master Chunyi Lin at his Wellness Center and at many SFQ events.  In the year 2011, Rhonda founded her company, Wonder Horse, LLC, to increase her reach and healing impact upon the community.

Saturday, February 3, 2018, Hospitality Hall (Church basement)

8:45am - Doors Open

9:00am - 11:30am  Session

Please RSVP by February 1 by calling the Parish Center at 612-823-8205 or by clicking on the box to the below.  Suggested Donation $5.

We look forward to seeing you!

Tip: Use Excel To Store and Access Content
That's right, it's time for another edition of "A tip or best practice we think you'll love!"
Here's a tip we think is so powerful that we've wrapped an entire webinar around it. Let me explain by way of example. Imagine building an information kiosk for a zoo with 50 animals. You could have one scene per animal but if you want to change the layout, you're modifying 50 different scenes. Did you know you could create a one scene template and read animal information from an Excel spreadsheet? Click the button below to see how to use this insanely powerful feature. It'll save you time and headaches.

Designing for Accessibility
Are your interactive experiences accessible to all people, regardless of ability? Have you even considered this question?
>From sight and hearing impairment to being chair bound or having a limited range of motion, your audience comes with a range of skills and challenges. If you'd like to build a truly accessible interactive experience, it needs to work for everyone, regardless of ability. Important Information 
 LivingSocial Account Update You are receiving this message based on your relationship with LivingSocial, even though you may have previously unsubscribed. If you have unsubscribed you will not receive any other messages from us. 
 IMPORTANT INFORMATION LivingSocial recently experienced a cyber-attack on our computer systems that resulted in unauthorized access to some customer data from our servers. We are actively working with law enforcement to investigate this issue. The information accessed includes names, email addresses, date of birth for some users, and encrypted passwords -- technically ‘hashed’ and ‘salted’ passwords. We never store passwords in plain text. Two things you should know: 
 The database that stores customer credit card information was not affected or accessed. 
 If you connect to LivingSocial using Facebook Connect, your Facebook credentials were not compromised. 
 You do not need to take any action at this time, but we wanted to be sure you were fully informed of what happened. The security of your information is our priority. We always strive to ensure the security of our customer information, and we are redoubling efforts to prevent any issues in the future. Please note that LivingSocial will never ask you directly for personal or account information in an email. We will always direct you to the LivingSocial website – and require you to login – before making any changes to your account. Please disregard any emails claiming to be from LivingSocial that request such information or direct you to a website that asks for such information. If you have additional questions about this process, the "Create New Password" button on LivingSocial.com will direct you to a page that has instructions on creating a new password and answers to frequently asked questions. We are sorry this incident occurred, and we look forward to continuing to introduce you to new and exciting things to do in your community. Sincerely, Tim O'Shaughness
